message:
feat(dashboard): add card selection and mouse click navigation

Enhances the dashboard page with interactive card selection supporting
both keyboard and mouse navigation:

DashboardCard enum:
- None, Services, Jobs, Deployments, Metrics variants
- target_page(): maps card to navigation target
- next()/prev(): keyboard cycling

CardBounds struct with RwLock:
- Stores rendered card coordinates for hit testing
- Uses interior mutability since view() takes &self
- Updated during render for accurate hit detection

Mouse support:
- MouseMsg handling for click events
- Hit testing against stored CardBounds
- Navigation on Enter or left-click when card selected

Keyboard navigation:
- Tab/Shift+Tab: cycle through cards
- Arrow keys: prev/next card
- Enter: navigate to card's target page

Visual feedback:
- Selected card gets highlight border
- Focus indicator for accessibility

test_support.rs adjustments for new dashboard state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1dr6","title":"demo_showcase page: Jobs/Tasks (async workflows, progress, filtering)","description":"A realistic CI/job-runner screen: a dense jobs list/table with progress, filtering, and actions that feel like a real ops console.

Why:
- This page is the best “integration crucible” for the stack:
  - bubbletea message routing + command composition
  - bubbles components (table/list, progress, spinner, paginator, timer/stopwatch)
  - harmonica motion for compelling progress/counter animation (optional)
  - charmed_log/toasts for outcomes and errors
- It proves the architecture scales beyond toy demos.

Must showcase (minimum):
- Navigation + selection:
  - keyboard-first (arrows/jk, enter), consistent focus model
  - selection stability across refreshes and filter changes
- Status + progress:
  - determinate progress bars and indeterminate spinners
  - color-coded statuses (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
  - durations + ETA-ish hints (timer/stopwatch)
- Realistic operations:
  - start/cancel/retry actions implemented via Cmd/AsyncCmd
  - action results visible (toast + log + UI state)
- Discoverability:
  - query bar + filtering + sorting
  - paginator for large lists
- Details pane:
  - shows job metadata, last logs, parameters, timeline / steps

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine and seeded generator.
- Integrates with Wizard: wizard triggers new jobs; jobs correlate to logs.

Acceptance:
- Feels like an actual CI dashboard, not a toy.
- Actions are responsive: input never blocks, state updates are obvious.
- Filtering is fast and predictable.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filtering/sorting correctness and stability
  - action state machine (start/cancel/retry) transitions
  - progress rendering decisions (spinner vs bar, colors)
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - smoke tour coverage
  - optional “jobs filtering” scenario if needed for regression.

Notes:
- Keep the information density high, but never sacrifice clarity.
- Make sure `--no-animations` still looks good (no “blank” experience).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.395269743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:41.424387508Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dr6","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.423951863Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxb","title":"Logs: filtering + search (level/service/query)","description":"Add filtering + search controls to the Logs page.

Why:
- Logs are only useful when you can slice them quickly.
- This is also a great way to showcase bubbles textinput + the app’s focus/keybinding model.

Filters to implement:
- Level selector (e.g., TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
- Service selector (from demo domain model)
- Free-text query (substring or simple matcher)

Performance requirements:
- Filtering should feel instant.
- Avoid re-rendering/parsing the entire log history on every keystroke.
- Prefer an incremental or pre-indexed filter model that can be unit tested.

UX/polish:
- Highlight matches in the viewport (optional).
- Bracketed paste should work in the query input.
- Show an inline summary: "123 lines (12 matches)".

Acceptance:
- Filtering feels instant and scales to a large buffer.
- Covered by unit tests at the filtering-engine layer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.782877999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352469043Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352332649Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.799160009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:41.631552397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:39.044747386Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:33.366234822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Scenario outline:
1) Launch demo_showcase in deterministic headless mode.
2) Navigate to Files.
3) Use the file picker to:
   - move selection
   - enter a directory (if supported)
   - select a file
4) Validate preview behavior:
   - preview pane becomes non-empty
   - breadcrumb / filename reflects the selected entry
   - selecting a markdown fixture renders via glamour (not raw markdown), using the current theme

Notes:
- Avoid relying on the host filesystem. Use the demo’s deterministic fixture directory (from the asset strategy / Config).

Acceptance:
- Deterministic in CI.
- Failure artifacts include the directory listing, selected filename, and the last rendered Files frame.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.191594688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:01.511508193Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","files","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-1wtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.230688217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-22c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:58.608108725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.221867959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.217633407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.226320857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.213212187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Maintain backward compatibility where practical (but no shims).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:48.923880193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.451539959Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.450257984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed modes/flags (finalize based on UX concept):
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--theme-file <path>` (optional)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (run without alt-screen; useful for debugging + demonstrating bubbletea println/printf)
- `--self-check` (headless CI-friendly render + exit)

Optional subcommands:
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (wish integration; feature-gated)
- `export --html <path>` / `export --plain <path>` (optional: export snapshots)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory.
- Flags map directly to `demo_showcase::Config` fields.
- Error messages are user-friendly (bad theme name, bad seed, missing file).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539935175Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539870655Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884573066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.830556320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1isw","title":"huh: Fix FilePicker single-file selection edge case","description":"# Bug: FilePicker Cannot Select Single File

## Problem Statement
In the FilePicker navigation logic, the down-arrow condition prevents
selecting the only file when there's exactly one file:

```rust
// Lines 3660-3663
if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1) {
    self.selected_index += 1;
    // ...
}
```

When files.len() == 1:
- saturating_sub(1) = 0
- condition: 0 < 0 = FALSE
- navigation does nothing

## Root Cause Analysis
The condition `< len - 1` is meant to prevent going past the last item,
but it also prevents selecting the first (and only) item when starting
from index 0.

Actually, the bug is in the logic design. The condition should allow
moving TO the last item, not just items BEFORE the last.

Wait, re-reading: if selected_index is 0 and len is 1:
- Can't move down because 0 < 0 is false
But the item at index 0 IS selectable - it's already selected by default.

The real issue might be different - let me trace the full logic.

## Actual Issue
The condition prevents moving DOWN when already at last item, which is correct.
But if the initial state has selected_index pointing elsewhere or if there's
a reset that doesn't account for single-item lists, selection could be broken.

Let me re-examine:
```rust
// When len=1, saturating_sub(1)=0
// selected_index starts at 0 (presumably)
// Down key: 0 < 0 is false, no change (correct - already at end)
// Up key: 0 > 0 is false, no change (correct - already at start)
```

This is actually correct for navigation. The bug might be elsewhere -
perhaps in initialization or the actual selection/confirm logic.

## Further Investigation Needed
1. Check initial selected_index value for single-file directories
2. Check if Enter/confirm works with single file
3. Check if the file is visually highlighted

## Potential Fix Areas
If init doesn't set selected_index correctly:
```rust
fn set_files(&mut self, files: Vec<PathBuf>) {
    self.files = files;
    self.selected_index = 0.min(self.files.len().saturating_sub(1));
}
```

If confirm doesn't work:
```rust
fn confirm(&self) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    self.files.get(self.selected_index).cloned()
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn filepicker_single_file() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/single-file-dir");
    // Simulate single file
    picker.files = vec![PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")];
    picker.selected_index = 0;
    
    // Should be able to select it
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("only_file.txt"));
    assert!(/* file is highlighted */);
    
    // Confirm should return the file
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert_eq!(selected, Some(PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")));
}

#[test]
fn filepicker_empty_directory() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/empty-dir");
    picker.files = vec![];
    
    // Should handle gracefully
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("No files") || view.is_empty() == false);
    
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert!(selected.is_none());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-file directories work correctly
- [ ] Empty directories handled gracefully
- [ ] Navigation works for 0, 1, and many files
- [ ] Selection/confirm works in all cases
- [ ] Tests added for edge cases","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.943176512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967235547Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","filepicker","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1isw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967192347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32pp","title":"Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log","description":"Implement the *styled* log formatting layer for the Logs page using `charmed_log`.

Why:
- A flagship ops-style demo lives and dies by log readability.
- This showcases that `charmed_log` + lipgloss styling can produce beautiful, information-dense output.

Formatting requirements:
- Each log entry should include (where available):
  - timestamp (compact)
  - level (TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
  - service/component name
  - message
- Styling:
  - color-coded levels (warn/error pop, info muted)
  - consistent column alignment and truncation
  - preserve legibility in narrow terminals
- No-color mode:
  - degrade gracefully (no ANSI), still aligned and readable

Integration:
- Logs are produced by the demo domain simulation and by user actions.
- The formatted line should carry the structured fields needed for filtering (level/service/query) without re-parsing the rendered string.

Acceptance:
- Logs look great, are readable at a glance, and remain usable with thousands of lines.
- Deterministic formatting (important for snapshot/E2E artifacts).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:37.574456901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801798652Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801717932Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogFormatter with styled, aligned output supporting color-coded levels, no-color mode, and configurable column widths. Added 8 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:37.594269536Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-33fe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:28.585526827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:31.153270873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33fe","title":"demo_showcase data: structured log stream + formatting","description":"Create the underlying structured log stream used by the demo (domain simulation + user actions).

Why:
- The Logs page should not be "just strings". We want structured fields so filtering/search is fast and correct.
- It proves the integration story: domain layer emits events, UI renders + filters them.

Deliverables:
- A `LogEntry` type (or similar) with fields like:
  - timestamp / monotonic tick
  - level
  - service/component
  - message
  - optional correlation ids (job id, deployment id)
- A log buffer/stream:
  - append-only collection with a bounded retention policy (optional)
  - API to push new entries from simulation/actions

Integration:
- The Logs viewer uses this stream and a formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`) to render.
- Filtering operates on structured fields, not re-parsed rendered text.

Acceptance:
- Large log volumes remain responsive.
- Entries support correlation (wizard → job → logs) and filtering by level/service.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:53.990998123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336843390Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336748333Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured log stream with filtering. Enhanced LogEntry with tick, job_id, deployment_id correlation. Created LogStream with filtering by level/target/job/deployment/text/tick-range, count_by_level(), and bounded retention. Added 16 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:54.007146912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:48.231233898Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Proves cross-page integration and the domain action API.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.600509120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:41:35.850246365Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:41:35.850177547Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-1x3q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:30.270047334Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-2qq7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:27.941778058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:25.630436696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:31.585436762Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.630180261Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35d2","title":"E2E: headless scripted runner (custom IO + recorded inputs)","description":"Implement an E2E runner that can drive the app without a real TTY.

Core approach:
- Run the app in custom I/O mode (bubbletea `with_input`/`with_output`) so it does not touch the real terminal.
- Feed deterministic input into the program:
  - Prefer a semantic step DSL (key presses, text input, resize, mouse click/scroll).
  - Optionally support raw byte streams to exercise the InputParser explicitly.
- Capture rendered frames (strings) so tests can assert on UI output.
- Terminate deterministically (send quit key / QuitMsg; enforce a max-step guard).

Time determinism:
- Avoid wall-clock sleeps.
- Advance simulation/animations by injecting tick messages and/or running a fixed "frame pump" loop.
- Ensure scenarios can run with animations disabled to reduce flakiness.

Assertions:
- Provide helpers for:
  - `assert_contains("Jobs")` / `assert_not_contains("\x1b[")` (no-color)
  - page header checks
  - optional direct AppState assertions (if the runner has access to the final model)

Integration:
- Must plug into the structured artifact logger (`E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture`).

Acceptance:
- Scenarios are readable scripts: "press j", "open docs", "search", "quit".
- Runner failures explain *where* and *why* (step id + last frame + config snapshot).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.886754241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:16:04.460032517Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:16:04.459867219Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37.168687580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:03.434318654Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:05.951709495Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:04.674114434Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.910218872Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:07.191517307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3b7o","title":"Settings: keybindings reference + customization hooks","description":"Display a comprehensive keybindings reference inside Settings (and optionally link to it from the help overlay).

Why:
- The demo must be self-teaching. Users should never feel stuck or need to read source.
- A single canonical keymap also prevents "random per-page keys" that make TUIs feel amateurish.

What to show:
- Global keys (quit, help, back/close, focus next/prev, search/command palette, theme switch, guided tour).
- Page-specific shortcuts (Dashboard drill-down, Jobs start/cancel, Logs follow, Docs search, Files open/preview).
- Interaction conventions:
  - list/table navigation
  - viewport scrolling
  - form submit/cancel

Nice-to-have:
- Show the *effective* keymap (reflecting feature flags/toggles):
  - if command palette not built, omit it
  - if mouse disabled, hide mouse tips

Acceptance:
- The key reference is readable, nicely styled, and matches the actual behavior.
- The app feels self-teaching: new users can explore without guessing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:05.551180539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:24.199337985Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3b7o","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:10.878758538Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b7o","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:05.585413730Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b7o","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:08.241305359Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3chy","title":"demo_showcase: optional SSH server mode (wish)","description":"Add an optional mode that serves the showcase over SSH (via `wish`) to prove the remote/SSH story is viable.

Why:
- Wish is one of the Charm ecosystem’s most compelling differentiators.
- Serving the same UI over SSH demonstrates composability: bubbletea Program IO abstraction + wish sessions + terminal capability negotiation.

Requirements:
- CLI:
  - `demo_showcase --ssh <addr>` (e.g., `:2222` or `0.0.0.0:2222`) starts the server.
  - host key UX:
    - accept `--host-key <path>` and/or generate a key if missing (decide in child tasks)
- Session behavior:
  - each SSH connection gets its own app instance (or a curated subset if full UI is too heavy)
  - wire bubbletea input/output to the SSH session streams
  - handle PTY sizing and resize events (best-effort)
- Security/ergonomics:
  - basic auth/middleware polish (simple but not embarrassing)
  - clear logs + startup banner (address, auth mode, host key path)

Acceptance:
- Running the server and connecting with `ssh -p 2222 localhost` launches the UI and is usable.
- Works with minimal setup beyond a host key.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - argument parsing and config wiring for ssh mode
  - host-key path validation logic
- E2E scripts:
  - a CI-safe “handshake + banner” test (non-interactive) that proves the server starts and accepts connections.
  - a local “interactive smoke” script (optional) that drives basic navigation via SSH and captures logs/artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep this optional: the main value of demo_showcase should not depend on SSH being enabled.
- Be realistic about CI environments; gate the heavier SSH tests appropriately.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.593755071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:44:44.471178493Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ssh","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3chy","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.612205255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3d69","title":"bubbletea: Add debug logging for channel disconnection","description":"# Task: Add Debug Logging for Channel Disconnection

## Problem Statement
Throughout the event loop, channel send errors are silently discarded:

\`\`\`rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Error ignored
\`\`\`

When the main thread exits (panic, quit, etc.), spawned command threads
continue executing but their results disappear into the void. There's no
logging or visibility into these "orphaned" command executions.

## Affected Locations
- Line 610: External message forwarding
- Line 628: Custom input parser
- Line 680-690: Event messages
- Line 838: Batch command results
- Line 847: Sequence command results
- Line 852: Normal command results

## Impact
- **Debugging difficulty**: Hard to diagnose when commands execute but effects don't appear
- **Resource waste**: Commands continue executing after they're no longer needed
- **Silent failures**: No indication that something went wrong

## Solution Design

### Use tracing for Debug Logging
\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{debug, warn, instrument};

// Replace:
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);

// With:
if let Err(e) = tx_clone.send(msg) {
    debug!(
        target: "bubbletea::command",
        error = %e,
        command_type = ?cmd_type,
        "Command result dropped - receiver disconnected"
    );
}
\`\`\`

### Structured Logging Fields
\`\`\`rust
struct CommandContext {
    command_id: u64,
    command_type: &'static str,
    batch_id: Option<u64>,
}

// Log with context
debug!(
    target: "bubbletea",
    command_id = ctx.command_id,
    command_type = ctx.command_type,
    batch_id = ?ctx.batch_id,
    "Command result dropped"
);
\`\`\`

### Log Levels
- **trace**: All channel sends (very verbose, for deep debugging)
- **debug**: Failed sends during normal operation
- **warn**: Failed sends that indicate unexpected state

## Implementation Steps
1. Add tracing feature flag (already present)
2. Create helper function for logged send
3. Replace all \`let _ = tx.send()\` with logged version
4. Add command context tracking where feasible
5. Add documentation example
6. Add integration test for log capture

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
use tracing_test::traced_test;

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn channel_disconnection_is_logged() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    drop(rx);  // Disconnect receiver
    
    // This should log at debug level
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result dropped"));
    assert!(logs_contain("test_command"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn successful_send_logs_at_trace_level() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    // Trace level logs successful sends
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result sent"));
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Test
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn logs_include_command_context() {
    use tracing_subscriber::layer::SubscriberExt;
    
    let (captured, guard) = setup_log_capture();
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel);
    let cmd = Command::new(|| {
        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
        Some(Message::new("delayed"))
    });
    
    program.send(cmd);
    program.quit();  // Quit before command completes
    
    // Command result should be logged as dropped
    let logs = captured.into_inner();
    assert!(logs.iter().any(|l| l.contains("dropped")));
}
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: bubbletea_logging_e2e.sh

set -e
echo "=== bubbletea Channel Logging Tests ==="

# Enable trace logging
export RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug

# Run tests with log capture
cargo test --package bubbletea channel_disconnection -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log

# Verify expected log messages appear
if grep -q "Command result dropped" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Channel disconnection logging works"
else
    echo "[FAIL] Channel disconnection not logged"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify structured fields present
if grep -q "command_type" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Structured logging fields present"
else
    echo "[WARN] Missing structured fields - check log format"
fi

echo "=== Test Complete ==="
\`\`\`

## Documentation Example
\`\`\`rust
//! # Debugging bubbletea Command Execution
//!
//! Enable debug logging to see command execution flow:
//!
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! For even more detail:
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=trace cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! ## Log Message Reference
//!
//! | Level | Message | Meaning |
//! |-------|---------|---------|
//! | trace | "Command result sent" | Normal operation |
//! | debug | "Command result dropped" | Receiver disconnected |
//! | warn | "Command failed during active loop" | Unexpected |
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All channel send failures logged at debug level
- [ ] Logs include command context (type, id)
- [ ] Structured logging with tracing
- [ ] No performance impact when logging disabled
- [ ] Unit tests with tracing_test
- [ ] Integration test for log capture
- [ ] E2E test script validates logging
- [ ] Documentation example added","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.262130512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:33:29.920025821Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","error-handling","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d69","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.289804229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d87","title":"Docs: syntax highlighting + line numbers toggle","description":"Add syntax highlighting and optional line numbers to rendered Markdown in the Docs page.

Why:
- Syntax highlighting is a flagship Glamour feature and a major "wow" factor.
- It also demonstrates that the demo respects runtime toggles and terminal capabilities.

Requirements:
- Highlight fenced code blocks when the feature is enabled.
- Optional line numbers (toggle-able):
  - consistent width/padding
  - do not break wrapping
- Must respect:
  - no-color / ASCII mode (disable highlighting cleanly)
  - Config/Settings toggles (syntax on/off, line numbers on/off)

Performance:
- Avoid re-highlighting on every keystroke; only re-render on relevant changes (doc switch, resize, theme/toggles).

Acceptance:
- Code blocks look great and readable.
- Toggling syntax/line numbers is immediate and stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:45.651163291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:45:00.461899225Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:45:00.461832531Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","glamour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d87","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:45.667314519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d87","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:36.620689828Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ecr","title":"E2E scenario: settings toggles + theme switching","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that validates Settings actually changes the running app.

Scenario outline (headless/custom-IO):
1) Launch demo_showcase in a deterministic mode (fixed seed, animations off).
2) Navigate to Settings.
3) Toggle at least:
   - no-color / ASCII mode (or equivalent)
   - mouse enable (even if headless, ensure toggle state updates)
4) Switch theme preset and confirm global restyling takes effect.
5) Return to Dashboard (or another page) and confirm the changes persist across navigation.

Assertions:
- Output changes in expected ways (e.g., theme name label updates, ANSI sequences absent when no-color).
- No panics and no hangs.

Acceptance:
- Scenario runs deterministically in CI.
- On failure, the artifact bundle clearly shows the last page and the toggle/theme state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PurpleCastle","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.112857002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:12:39.341588186Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.145700883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.141257332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150092478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.137009755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.131740545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3eru","title":"Unit tests: filtering engines (jobs/logs/docs)","description":"Add focused unit tests for filtering/search logic (without spinning up the full UI).

Why:
- Filtering and search are easy to subtly break (case sensitivity, tokenization, pagination interactions).
- We want fast feedback during refactors.

Coverage:
- Jobs filtering/sorting:
  - query matches by name/id/service
  - status filters compose correctly
  - sorting is stable and deterministic
- Logs filtering:
  - level/service selectors work
  - free-text query matches expected fields
- Docs search:
  - match finding and next/prev navigation over a rendered buffer

Edge cases to include:
- empty query
- Unicode input
- very long query (no panic, bounded behavior)
- paste-like input containing newlines/whitespace

Nice-to-have:
- Add a small set of property tests (e.g., filter is idempotent, sorting preserves multiset) if it stays lightweight.

Acceptance:
- Core filter logic is correct and stays correct during refactors.
- Tests are deterministic and run fast.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.169792772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:26.390328143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:26.390262520Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-1fxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:26.759858058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-212j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:25.449597433Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:28.296789159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.182594415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

  - where it appears in the UI (page/workflow)
  - which automated test(s) cover it (unit/e2e/snapshot)
  - any manual-only checks (mouse drag, SSH interactive)

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-3if6","title":"Implement wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit)","description":"Implement Wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit) matching Go wish.

 {"id":"bd-3j9x","title":"demo_showcase: core TUI shell + router (bubbletea)","description":"Implement the reusable application shell that everything else plugs into.

Key ideas:
- One top-level App model that owns routing + global concerns (theme, status, notifications).
- Pages are sub-models/components; App delegates update/view and composes commands.
- Strong separation between domain data (fake backend) and presentation.

Acceptance:
- App boots into a multi-pane layout with navigation and at least one page.
- Clean message routing (no spaghetti downcasts everywhere).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.314919933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333853721Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j9x","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333820659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3jg2","title":"Interactions: bracketed paste support in inputs/search","description":"Bubbletea delivers paste sequences as `KeyMsg` with `paste=true` (and/or via terminal paste events).

Why:
- Paste is a "real app" interaction: users paste queries, IDs, paths, and commit hashes.
- Terminals can deliver large paste payloads; naïve handling can lag or break inputs.

Ensure paste works sensibly in:
- Jobs query bar
- Logs query/filter input
- Docs search
- Wizard text fields (huh)
- Notes scratchpad (textarea)

Edge cases:
- Very large paste:
  - should not freeze the UI
  - inputs should apply paste in a bounded way (e.g., respect char limits)
- Multi-line paste:
  - for search inputs: collapse whitespace/newlines into spaces (or clearly defined behavior)
  - for textarea: preserve newlines

Acceptance:
- Pasting a multi-word query "just works" across inputs.
- No hangs or panics with large paste payloads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.767936662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372890345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372758850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","paste"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.786648712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:58.203351507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
